content: Curiosity-driven reinforcement learning can be re-framed to intentionally search for new environmental signals that expand an agent’s working memory capacity incrementally during active learning, rather than only optimizing for task reward. This creates a self-adjusting cycle where an agent’s own exploratory behavior alleviates the working memory limits that constrain its reasoning over time.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
